records.title: Objective Coverage Audit
records.purpose: Maps the active datastore objective to current evidence, proven coverage, blockers, and non-completion rationale.
records.safe_content_policy: Operational metadata only; no raw source text, transcripts, or long excerpts.

records.title: Coverage Integrity Audit
records.purpose: Checks consistency across source manifests, local chapter records, source-map viewer data, and vault artifact paths.
records.safe_content_policy: Operational metadata only; no raw source text, transcripts, or long excerpts.

records.title: Source Map Path Integrity Audit
records.purpose: Checks source-map JSON and embedded HTML paths for vault-relative path style and target resolution.
records.safe_content_policy: Operational metadata only; no raw source text, transcripts, or long excerpts.

records.title: Wiki Link Integrity Audit
records.purpose: Checks Obsidian wiki-link target resolution across Markdown notes.
records.safe_content_policy: Navigation metadata only; not source-canon evidence.

records.title: Raw Text And Excerpt Safety Audit
records.purpose: Checks for raw source payload files, quote-heavy notes, and transcript/book-dump risk signals inside the vault.
records.safe_content_policy: Heuristic safety metadata only; not a legal opinion.

records.title: Local Book Coverage Granularity
records.purpose: Separates chapter-sweep parent books from targeted book-slice notes so downstream agents do not overclaim local-book coverage.
records.safe_content_policy: Coverage metadata only; no raw book text.

records.title: Stanford Current Availability Checks
records.purpose: Tracks official Stanford public-material availability, future slots, schedule-only entries, and gated video boundaries.
records.safe_content_policy: Availability metadata only; no video transcript or protected material.

records.title: Stanford Video Source Coverage Matrix
records.purpose: Separates public recording visibility, video-ingested status, public notes/slides, schedule-only entries, archive-year playlists, and gated recordings.
records.safe_content_policy: Video-source metadata only; no transcript or timestamp dump.

records.title: YouTube Playlist Worklist
records.purpose: Tracks official Stanford playlist pointers as navigation aids, not automatic video-understanding evidence.
records.safe_content_policy: Playlist metadata only; no transcript or timestamp dump.

records.title: Official/Open URL Coverage Priority
records.purpose: Summarizes URL-level coverage state and remaining URL-level priority gaps.
records.safe_content_policy: URL coverage metadata only; no copied webpage content.

records.title: Obsidian Ingestion Operating Model
records.purpose: Defines note-building workflow and guardrails for original synthesis, source attribution, and non-duplication.

records.title: Next Ingestion Queue
records.purpose: Machine-readable prioritized queue for blocked, future, optional, deferred, and recurring ingestion work.
records.safe_content_policy: Queue metadata only; not source-canon evidence and no copied source text.

records.title: Agent Studio Topic Coverage Matrix
records.purpose: Maps Agent Studio architecture domains to canon notes, source families, release gates, and concrete design uses.
records.safe_content_policy: Coverage metadata only; not source-canon evidence and no copied source text.

records.title: Deferred Local Corpus Queue
records.purpose: Tracks deferred, private, and question-backlog local files with allowed actions, trigger conditions, and blocked-by-default canon policy.
records.safe_content_policy: Queue metadata only; no raw local file text or excerpts.

records.title: Stanford Public Video Ingestion Status
records.purpose: Tracks official public Stanford CS25 recordings that are visible as YouTube videos, their metadata/caption availability, linked canon notes, and whether direct full-video watching has actually occurred.
records.safe_content_policy: Availability and metadata only; no video download, raw transcript, comments, timestamp dump, or long excerpt. Captions/transcripts/comments are not substitutes for watched-video coverage.
